Mohamad Hasan Sweidan:

During today's meeting with our people who were forced to leave their homes in the border villages, some of whom left home a year ago and live in one of Beirut's schools, this paper hanging on the wall of the school playground caught my attention:

"We are the sons of the steadfast South.
We pledge to your approach, Abu Hadi (Sayyed Nasrallah).
We will never deviate from your footsteps.
We pledge to be victorious, our brother and father.
We will resist... we will fight."

These words were not just phrases written on paper, but rather a living pulse of hearts that were not softened by hardships. I found that the people, despite the weight of displacement and the bitterness of separation, carried a greater concern: that the wing of the resistance would not be broken.

A man in his seventies told me: "We want nothing more than victory, no matter the cost!" Then a young man in his twenties repeated forcefully what the seventy-year-old had said with firmness and confidence.

The resistance here is not just a weapon in the hands of the men of God on the battlefields, but rather a spirit rooted in a society of sacrifices and loyalty.  It is an unbreakable momentum, and a steadfastness that knows no defeat.

"Indeed, Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their properties [in exchange] for that they will have Paradise. They fight in the cause of Allah, so they kill and are killed. [It is] a true promise [binding] upon Him."
